characters.name: Abbie Hoffman
characters.description: The subject of this article: Abbie Hoffmanis an activist who is seen at theanti-war rallyinWashington, D.C..

characters.name: Billy
characters.description:
The subject of this article: "What the hell's goin' on here?! Who is that?! Who is that?!"
- Billy after Forrest mistakenly punched him, thinking that he was trying to hurt Jenny as Jenny only hit her head against Billy's car window while trying to make out with him. BillywasJenny's date while she is at the university. Forrest is waiting in the rain to see Jenny when Billy drives up with her in the car; they begin to kiss and Jenny accidently hits her head against the car window while trying to make out with Billy to which Forrest mistakenly believes Billy is hurting her, so he punches Billy, who angrily drives away and breaks up with Jenny. Jenny is initially mad at Forrest, but invites him in and they have sex.  In a deleted scene Billy comes to the college to get Jenny for their date and Forrest sees Jenny and waves goodbye to her but when he sees Jenny and Billy kiss each other he drops his hand in silence.  Billy is played by Mark Matheisen.

characters.description: BubbaBasic infoFull nameBenjamin Buford BlueNicknameBubbaGenderMaleBornMarch 2, 1943 bayou La Batre Alabama u.s.DiedJune 7, 1967 (aged 24)VietnamAge24StatusDeceasedSocial infoEthnicityAfrican-AmericanOccupationAmerican SoldierAffiliationUnited States ArmyFamilyunnamed ancestorsBubba's father(father deceased)Mrs. Blue(mother)unnamed uncle2 younger brothers3 younger sistersFriendsForrest Gump(best friend),Tex (possibly),Cleveland (possibly)Dallas (possibly)EnemiesVietnam Soldiers. –Bubba Benjamin "Bubba" Buford Blue(March 2, 1943 — June 7, 1967) wasForrest Gump's companion during theVietnam War. He knew everything there was to know about the shrimping business, and directly inspired theBubba Gump Shrimp Company.

characters.name: Cleveland
characters.description: The subject of this article: Clevelandis a Detroit native who serves inFourth PlatoonunderLieutenant Dan Taylor. Cleveland was the platoon medic, as he wears the M5 medic bag on his back, the standard issue medical rucksack.

characters.name: Cleveland
characters.description: The subject of this article: Clevelandis a Detroit native who serves inFourth PlatoonunderLieutenant Dan Taylor. Cleveland was the platoon medic, as he wears the M5 medic bag on his back, the standard issue medical rucksack.

characters.name: Dallas
characters.description: The subject of this article: Dallaswas a Phoenix native who was a U.S. soldier who fought in theVietnam WarunderLieutenant Dan Taylor, During an ambush, many platoon mates are killed, butForrest Gumpcarries a wounded Dallas and other soldiers out of the jungle.  In the war hospital when Forrest watches a tv show on tv Dallas walks by and says “Gump, How can you watch that stupid s***t?! Turn it off!” To which Forrest does.

characters.name: Dallas
characters.description: The subject of this article: Dallaswas a Phoenix native who was a U.S. soldier who fought in theVietnam WarunderLieutenant Dan Taylor, During an ambush, many platoon mates are killed, butForrest Gumpcarries a wounded Dallas and other soldiers out of the jungle. characters.name: Drill Sergeant
characters.description: –The Drill Sergeant Thedrill sergeantis a character in theForrest Gumpfilm, portrayed by Afemo Omilami. He is the sergeant of the same platoonForrest Gumpis in when he enters the army. The drill sergeant first asks Forrest what his sole purpose in the army is, with Forrest responding "to do whatever you tell me, drill sergeant!". The drill sergeant calls Forrest a "goddamn genius" and says his response was "the most outstanding thing he's ever heard" and calls Forrest "goddamn gifted".

characters.name: Drill Sergeant
characters.description: –The Drill Sergeant Thedrill sergeantis a character in theForrest Gumpfilm, portrayed by Afemo Omilami. He is the sergeant of the same platoonForrest Gumpis in when he enters the army. The drill sergeant first asks Forrest what his sole purpose in the army is, with Forrest responding "to do whatever you tell me, drill sergeant!". The drill sergeant calls Forrest a "goddamn genius" and says his response was "the most outstanding thing he's ever heard" and calls Forrest "goddamn gifted".

characters.name: Gerald Ford
characters.description: The subject of this article: Gerald Fordwas the 38th President of the United States. He has no direct role in theForrest Gumpfilm, but thesecond attempt to assassinate himis shown on a television newscast being watched byForrest GumpandLieutenant Danaboard the shrimping boatJenny. In thenovel, his assasination attempt is not discussed. However, the Vice President ofRichard Nixonhas a very brief role, in which he is verbally abused by the President and excuses himself by saying"I have to go pee". Nixon had two Vice Presidents:Spiro T. Agnewand, later, Gerald Ford. The Vice President discussed is not explicitly named, but he is likely to be Ford.

characters.name: Frank Wills
characters.description: The subject of this article: Frank Willsis the security guard who discovered theWatergatebreak-in. In theForrest Gumpfilm, whileForrestis staying at the Watergate Hotel, he is disturbed by the light from flashlights in a room of the opposing building. He calls security to tell them that the people in the room may have suffered a broken fuse. Frank Wills responds to the call, and goes on to discover the scandal.

characters.name: George Wallace
characters.description: The subject of this article: George Corley Wallace, Jr.was the Governor of Alabama for two nonconsecutive terms and two consecutive terms: from 1963-1967, 1971-1979, and 1983-1987. In theForrest Gumpfilm, he is seen during his famousStand in the Schoolhouse Dooron June 11, 1963 at theUniversity of Alabama, attempting to block black students from entering the school. Wallace also had an unsuccessful run for the presidency, and an unsuccessful attempt was made to assassinate him, which left him paralyzed. Both of these events are discussed in the film.

characters.name: Tom Hanks
characters.description: The subject of this article: Tom Hanksis an American actor. He playedForrest Gumpinthe movie by the same name. He has no role in thenoveloff of which the movie was based; however, in the sequelGump & Co., he appears as a character, playing himself. Forrest Gumpmeets Tom Hanks atElaine'srestaurant inNew York City. Tom Hanks tells Forrest someone should write a movie about his life, and also says "Life is like a box of chocolates" - while this phrase is pervasive in thefilmand  media surrounding it, this was the first and only time it was uttered by any character in the novel canon.

characters.name: Jimmy Carter
characters.description: The subject of this article: Jimmy Carterwas the 39th President of the United States. He has no active role in theForrest Gumpfilm, however a news clip describing his collapse to heat exhaustion plays in the background of the Greenbowbarber shopasForrestruns past.

characters.name: John F. Kennedy
characters.description: The subject of this article: John Fitzgerald "Jack" Kennedy(May 29, 1917 – November 22, 1963) was the 35th President of the United States, from 1961 until his assassination in 1963. In theForrest Gumpfilm,Forrestmeets the President inWashington, D.C.to be congratulated when he is certified anAll-Americanfootball player for his performance at theUniversity of Alabama. Presented with free food and drink, Forrest indulges in (he estimates) 15 Dr. Peppers, so that when he is asked by the President on live television "How does it feel?" (implying his repetitive question "How does it feel to be an all-American?"), all he can think to say is "I got to pee". Later, Forrest laments on President Kennedy'sassassination, followed by that of his brotherRobert Kennedyin 1968. An autographed picture ofMarilyn Monroecan be seen in the White House during Forrest's visit, alluding to persistent rumors that Kennedy and Monroe had an affair.

characters.name: John F. Kennedy
characters.description: The subject of this article: John Fitzgerald "Jack" Kennedy(May 29, 1917 – November 22, 1963) was the 35th President of the United States, from 1961 until his assassination in 1963. characters.name: John Lennon
characters.description: The subject of this article: John Lennon (left) on the Dick Cavett Show with Forrest Gump. John Lennon(October 9th 1940 - December 8th 1980) was a British musician in the popular British GroupThe Beatles.Forrest Gumpmet John Lennon in theForrest Gumpfilmwhen he was invited to be onThe Dick Cavett Showafter returning home to the United States fromChinaplayingping-pong.

characters.name: John Lennon

characters.name: Lyndon B. Johnson
characters.description: The subject of this article: Lyndon B. Johnson(August 27, 1908 - January 22, 1973) was the 36th President of the United States. He awardedForrest Gumpthe CongressionalMedal of Honorfor his actions in theVietnam War. Shortly after issuing the award, Forrest drops his pants and shows Johnson his wounded buttock. In the novel, Johnson invites Forrest to eat. He gives him a brief tour of the White House: in theRose Gardenhe shows Forrest a scar of his on his belly and inquires about Forrest's wound. Forrest drops his pants and shows his wounded buttock, an event photographed by the press and plastered on numerous newspapers, to the chagrin ofColonel Gooch, Forrest's escort in Washington. Seriously though, Hari, don't you have kids to teach?

characters.name: Lyndon B. Johnson

characters.description: The subject of this article: Dan TaylorBasic infoFull nameDan TaylorNicknameLieutenant DanGenderMaleBornCirca 1942 - 1943AgeEarly-Mid 50s as of Forrest GumpStatusAliveSocial infoOccupationMilitary Officer (formerly)CEO ofBubba Gump Shrimp CompanyFamilyUnnamed Great 3x GrandfatherUnnamed Great 2x GrandfatherUnnamed Great GrandfatherUnnamed WifeFriendsForrest GumpProduction infoPortrayed byGary Sinise Lieutenant Dan Tayloris a character in theForrest Gumpnoveland  ensuingfilm. He was most likely born in 1942 or 1943. He wasForrest's platoon leader during theVietnam Warin 1967 and later becomes Forrest's shrimp boat partner in theBubba-Gump Shrimp Company. He is played by Gary Sinise.

characters.name: Forrest Gump Jr.
characters.description: Forrest Alexander Gump, Jr.Basic infoBornApril 4, 1977Age4 (1981)Social infoFamilyForrest Gump- fatherJenny Curran- mother (deceased)Gretchen Gump- adoptive motherMrs. Gump- paternal grandmother (deceased)Mr. Gump- paternal grandfather (deceased)Mr. Curran- maternal grandfather (deceased)Mrs. Curran- maternal grandmother (deceased)Jenny's four other sisters- maternal aunts (unknown)Little ForrestorForrest Alexander Gump, Jr.(referred to in the film asForrest Jr.) is a character in theForrest Gumpnovel and film. He is the son of Forrest Gump and Jenny Curran and is played by Haley Joel Osment in the movie. The director of the movie, Robert Zemeckis, casted Osment to play Forrest Jr due to his role in a Pizza Hut commercial. In the movie, Forrest Gump Jr. first appears towards the end of the movie when Forrest comes to visit Jenny in Georgia, who is now a single mother. As Forrest and Jenny are talking, there's a knock at the door, to which a woman drops off a little boy to Jenny. To which Jenny introduces the little boy to Forrest. Then he asks Jenny if he can go watch T.V. She says yes but to keep it low. Jenny tells Forrest that her son is named Forrest. Also because she named him after his daddy to which Forrest asks her if she knows another man named Forrest. Which Jenny replies, "You're his daddy Forrest." (From when Forrest and Jenny had sex before she left and Forrest ran across America) Forrest Sr. is put into a state of shock, but quickly recovers and begins to accept paternity. He asks if the boy has inherited the mild retardation that plagued him, to which Jenny says there is no sign of that, and  that Forrest Jr is one of the smartest kids in his class. Forrest then goes into the room to watchSesame Streetwith his son and begins bonding with him. The next day Forrest and Jenny take Forrest Jr to the park. While Forrest Jr swings on the swing, his parents sit on the bench nearby and Jenny tells Forrest Sr about being sick. She decides to move with Forrest Jr to Forrest's house in Alabama and marry him. Shortly afterwards, Forrest Jr is seen walking with his parents back home and gathering leaves along the way. After Jenny dies and Forrest Sr visits her grave. He tells her that Forrest Jr is doing fine and that he's good a lot of things such as combing his hair, brushing his teeth, playing ping-pong, fishing, and reading and that he's really so smart and that he's so proud of him. Forrest also says that Forrest Jr wrote Jenny a letter so he leaves it on Jenny's grave. In the end Forrest Sr and Forrest Jr are waiting at the bus stop for Forrest Jr's school bus.  Forrest Jr tells his dad that he's sharing his bookCurious Georgebecause his Grandma (Mrs. Gump) used to read it to his dad when he was little. The bus then arrives but before Forrest Jr leaves, he and his dad tell each other that they love each other. Forrest Jr introduces himself to the bus driver Dorothy Harris, who drove his dad to school when he was a kid. Then, Forrest Jr. g

characters.name: Major Fritch
characters.description: The subject of this article: Major Janet Fritchis America's top secret first female astronaut. She accompaniesForrest Gumpand chimpanzeeSueon theMulti-Orbital Pre-Planetary Sub-Gravitational Inter-Spheroid Spaceflight Training Mission. Major Fritch eventually moves in with a tribesman namedGrurck, after he pulled her into a bush and presumably ravished her.

characters.name: Mike
characters.description: The subject of this article: MikeintroducesForrest Gumpto the world ofprofessional wrestling, creates the persona of The Dunce, and organizes matches (predetermining the winner and loser). Later in the novel, he is employed in Forrest'sshrimpingbusiness, placed in charge of public relations and advertising.

characters.name: Miss French
characters.description: The subject of this article: Miss Frenchwas an operator for the telephone company who stayed at the Gump family home as a boarder. One stormy night soon afterForrest's graduation from high school, Miss French invites Forrest into her room withdivinities, and does sexual "things" to him. The event convinces Forrest to pursue a relationship withJenny Curran. Later in the book, while Forrest is inVietnamrecovering from his gunshot wound, he receives a letter from his mother saying that while Miss French was drying her cat with a hair dryer, she started a fire which burned down the Gump family home.

characters.name: Mister Gump
characters.description: The subject of this article: Forrest's fatheris never depicted. Thenovelgoes into detail about his death:

characters.name: Mister Tribble
characters.description: The subject of this article: Mister Tribbleis a former international grand master inchess, andForrest Gump's mentor. Forrest finds Mister Tribble playing chess with himself outside the Grandmaster's Invitational Chess Tournament, and joins in as the second player mid-game. He easily defeats Mister Tribble, who is impressed and convinces him to attend the international chess championship in Los Angeles. Forrest wins the championship, but embarrasses himself with flatulence, and Mister Tribble cuts off their partnership.

characters.name: Mister Wilkins
characters.description: The subject of this article: Mister WilkinsisForrest Gump's companion in Communist China when he visits to playping-pong. Wilkins is strict about etiquette with the Chinese, and criticizes Forrest for being unkempt.

characters.name: Mr. Curran
characters.description: Mr. CurranBasic infoNicknameDaddy, Grandpa,GenderMaleBornMay 11, 1908DiedMay 11, 1955Age47 (at death)StatusDeceasedSocial infoResidencePrisonOccupationPrisonerFamilyUnnamed MotherUnnamed FatherJenny Curran(daughter; deceased)Unnamed DaughtersForrest Gump(Son in Law)Forrest Gump Jr(Grandson)Unnamed BrothersUnnamed SistersMrs. Curran(Wife)EnemiesJenny CurranForrest GumpProduction infoPortrayed byKevin Mangan Mr. Curran(born May 11, 1908 - died May 11, 1955) was a minor character in theForrest Gumpfilm. He was the father ofJenny Curranand her sisters, the father-in-law ofForrest Gump, and the maternal grandfather ofForrest Gump Jr.He is played by Kevin Mangan, a crew member who worked in the movie's art department. Mr. Curran does not appear in thenovelwhich preceded the film. Jenny's mother,Mrs. Curran, plays a role, however.

Mr. Curran does not appear in thenovelwhich preceded the film. Jenny's mother,Mrs. Curran, plays a role, however.

characters.name: Mr. Hancock
characters.description: The subject of this article: Mr. Hancockis a minor character inForrest Gump. He is principal of Greenbow County Central School and is played by actor Sam Anderson in the movie. In the movie Mr. Hancock appears in the scene where Forrest's Mother takes Forrest to school because she wants him to have the finest education in the world. While Forrest waits outside on a bench, his mother and Mr. Hancock talk inside his office.

characters.name: Mr. Hancock

While Forrest waits outside on a bench, his mother and Mr. Hancock talk inside his office.

characters.name: Mrs. Blue
characters.description: The subject of this article:  Mrs. Blueis a character in the filmForrest Gump. She is the mother of Benjamin Buford "Bubba" Blue and 5 other children. She is played by Marlena Smalls in the movie. She is not depicted in thenovel, in place ofBubba's father.

characters.name: Mrs. Blue
characters.description: The subject of this article:  Mrs. Blueis a character in the filmForrest Gump. She is the mother of Benjamin Buford "Bubba" Blue and 5 other children. She is played by Marlena Smalls in the movie. She is not depicted in thenovel, in place ofBubba's father.

characters.name: Mrs. Gump
characters.description: Mrs. GumpBasic infoFull nameMrs. GumpBornNovember 7th 1907DiedMarch 23rd 1976Age68StatusDeceased.Mrs. Gump(played bySally Field) isForrest Gump’s mother,Jenny’s mother-in-law,Mister Gump’s wife and Forrest Jr.’s grandmother in theForrest Gumpnovelandfilm. She makes every effort to ensure his success, and makes wise quotes, which Forrest repeats throughout his life. She later died of what is believed to be cancer. When Forrest Gump was a baby, Mrs. Gump named him afterNathan Bedford Forrest, Founder of the Ku Klux Klan, as a reminder that "sometimes we all do things that just don't make no sense." Mrs. Gump always defends Forrest, including yelling at the old men when Forrest’s leg brace got stuck in the grate, and whenMr. Hancock, the principal said Forrest was “different.” However, to get her son into the public school system, she had sex with the principal.

She makes every effort to ensure his success, and makes wise quotes, which Forrest repeats throughout his life. She later died of what is believed to be cancer. characters.name: Richard Nixon
characters.description: The subject of this article: Forrest Gump Watergate Richard Milhous Nixon(January 9, 1913 - April 22, 1994) was the 37th President of the United States from 1969 until his resignation in 1974 and is the only U.S. President to resign the presidency as a result of the Watergate scandal. Nixon appears in both versions ofForrest Gump.

characters.name: Raquel Welch
characters.description: The subject of this article: An iconic image of Welch from the 1966 movieOne Million Years B.C.This image is not from any member of theForrest Gumpuniverse. Raquel Welchwas an American actress and sex symbol. She appears in theForrest Gumpnovelas an actor in a remake ofThe Creature from the Black Lagoon. While filming a scene, Raquel's clothing is ripped off, andForrest Gump, wearing a rubber monster suit, as well asSuethe chimpanzee, set off to find clothes for her.
